001|# Odoo Online Customer Portal Redesign PRD
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python (Odoo framework), QWeb, SCSS, JS
006|- **Project Name:** odoo_online_customer_portal
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Redesign the Odoo Online customer portal UI using QWeb, SCSS, and JS.
009|  - Integrate legal compliance features: Terms & Conditions checkbox, customer consent storage, and necessary frontend/backend modifications.
010|
011|## 2. Product Definition
012|### Product Goals
013|1. Modernize the customer portal UI for improved usability and aesthetics.
014|2. Ensure legal compliance by capturing and storing customer consent for Terms & Conditions.
015|3. Seamlessly integrate new features into both frontend and backend with minimal disruption.
016|
017|### User Stories
018|- As a customer, I want to easily navigate the portal so that I can access my account and services efficiently.
019|- As a customer, I want to see and accept Terms & Conditions before proceeding so that my consent is recorded for legal compliance.
020|- As an admin, I want to view and manage customer consent records so that I can ensure regulatory requirements are met.
021|- As a developer, I want the UI components to be modular and maintainable so that future updates are easier.
022|- As a compliance officer, I want to verify that consent storage meets legal standards so that the business avoids penalties.
023|
024|### Competitive Analysis
025|| Product                | Pros                                      | Cons                                      |
026||------------------------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
027|| Odoo Enterprise Portal | Native integration, robust features        | UI less modern, limited compliance tools  |
028|| Zoho Customer Portal   | Customizable, compliance modules           | Less flexible backend, higher cost        |
029|| Salesforce Experience  | Advanced UI, strong compliance features    | Complex setup, expensive                  |
030|| Freshdesk Portal       | User-friendly, GDPR tools                  | Limited backend customization             |
031|| SuiteCRM Portal        | Open source, customizable                  | UI outdated, compliance features limited  |
032|| HubSpot Service Hub    | Modern UI, consent management              | Expensive, less backend flexibility       |
033|| SAP Customer Portal    | Enterprise-grade, compliance ready         | High cost, complex integration            |
034|
035|### Competitive Quadrant Chart
036|```mermaid
037|quadrantChart
038|    title "Customer Portal UI vs. Compliance Features"
039|    x-axis "Basic UI" --> "Advanced UI"
040|    y-axis "Low Compliance" --> "High Compliance"
041|    quadrant-1 "UI Focus"
042|    quadrant-2 "Compliance Leaders"
043|    quadrant-3 "Legacy Systems"
044|    quadrant-4 "Balanced Solutions"
045|    "Odoo Enterprise Portal": [0.4, 0.5]
046|    "Zoho Customer Portal": [0.6, 0.6]
047|    "Salesforce Experience": [0.9, 0.9]
048|    "Freshdesk Portal": [0.7, 0.8]
049|    "SuiteCRM Portal": [0.3, 0.4]
050|    "HubSpot Service Hub": [0.8, 0.7]
051|    "SAP Customer Portal": [0.7, 0.9]
052|    "Our Target Product": [0.7, 0.8]
053|```
054|
055|## 3. Technical Specifications
056|### Requirements Analysis
057|- **Frontend:**
058|  - Redesign UI using QWeb templates, SCSS for styling, and JS for interactivity.
059|  - Add Terms & Conditions checkbox to registration and relevant forms.
060|  - Display consent status to users and admins.
061|- **Backend:**
062|  - Store customer consent in the database (linked to user profile).
063|  - Provide admin interface for consent management and reporting.
064|  - Ensure audit trail for consent changes.
065|- **Legal Compliance:**
066|  - Ensure consent capture meets GDPR and other relevant standards.
067|  - Allow users to review and update consent.
068|
069|### Requirements Pool
070|- **P0 (Must-have):**
071|  - Modern UI redesign (QWeb, SCSS, JS)
072|  - Terms & Conditions checkbox
073|  - Consent storage in backend
074|  - Admin consent management
075|- **P1 (Should-have):**
076|  - Audit trail for consent changes
077|  - User ability to review/update consent
078|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**
079|  - Customizable Terms & Conditions per region
080|  - Consent analytics dashboard
081|
082|### UI Design Draft
083|- **Layout:**
084|  - Dashboard with navigation sidebar
085|  - Profile page with consent status
086|  - Registration/login forms with T&C checkbox
087|  - Admin panel for consent management
088|
089|### Open Questions
090|- What legal jurisdictions must be supported (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)?
091|- Should consent be versioned if Terms & Conditions change?
092|- What reporting features are required for compliance audits?
093|- Is multi-language support required for T&C?

   +id: int\\n        +name: str\\n        +email: str\\n        +consent_ids: list[CustomerConsent]\\n    }\\n    class CustomerConsent {\\n        +id: int\\n        +user_id: int\\n        +consent_given: bool\\n        +consent_date: datetime\\n        +terms_version: str\\n        +jurisdiction: str\\n        +audit_log: list[ConsentAudit]\\n        +__init__(user_id: int, consent_given: bool, terms_version: str, jurisdiction: str)\\n    }\\n    class ConsentAudit {\\n        +id: int\\n        +consent_id: int\\n        +action: str\\n        +timestamp: datetime\\n        +performed_by: int\\n        +__init__(consent_id: int, action: str, performed_by: int)\\n    }\\n    class PortalController {\\n        +show_portal()\\n        +submit_consent(user_id: int, consent_given: bool, terms_version: str, jurisdiction: str)\\n        +get_consent_status(user_id: int) -> CustomerConsent\\n    }\\n    class AdminConsentController {\\n        +list_consents()\\n        +view_consent(consent_id: int)\\n        +update_consent(consent_id: int, consent_given: bool)\\n        +get_audit_trail(consent_id: int) -> list[ConsentAudit]\\n    }\\n    ResUsers \\"1\\" -- \\"*\\" CustomerConsent : has\\n    CustomerConsent \\"1\\" -- \\"*\\" ConsentAudit : logs\\n    PortalController ..> CustomerConsent : manages\\n    AdminConsentController ..> CustomerConsent : manages\\n    AdminConsentController ..> ConsentAudit : audits\\n```\\n\\n## Program call flow:\\n\\n```mermaid\\nsequenceDiagram\\n    participant U as User\\n    participant PC as PortalController\\n    participant CC as CustomerConsent\\n    participant AC as AdminConsentController\\n    participant CA as ConsentAudit\\n    participant DB as Database\\n\\n    U->>PC: Access portal (show_portal)\\n    PC->>DB: Fetch user profile\\n    PC->>CC: get_consent_status(user_id)\\n    CC-->>PC: Return consent status\\n    PC-->>U: Display dashboard/profile with consent info\\n\\n    U->>PC: Submit registration with T&C checkbox\\n    PC->>CC: submit_consent(user_id, consent_given, terms_version, jurisdiction)\\n    CC->>DB: Store consent record\\n    CC->>CA: Log audit entry (action=\\"consent_given\\")\\n    CA->>DB: Store audit log\\n    CC-->>PC: Confirmation\\n    PC-->>U: Show success message\\n\\n    participant Admin as Admin\\n    Admin->>AC: list_consents()\\n    AC->>DB: Query all consent records\\n    DB-->>AC: Return consent list\\n    AC-->>Admin: Display consent management panel\\n\\n    Admin->>AC: view_consent(consent_id)\\n    AC->>CC: Fetch consent entry (action=\\"consent_updated\\")\\n    CA->>DB: Store audit log\\n    AC-->>Admin: Confirmation\\n```\\n\\n## Anything UNCLEAR\\n\\n- Legal jurisdictions to support (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) need Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! When editing a file, make sure to insert appropriate indentation before each line! Ensuring the code adheres to PEP8 standards. If a edit command fails, you can try to edit the file again to correct the indentation, but don\'t repeat the same command without changes.\n6. To avoid syntax errors when editing files multiple times, consider opening the file to view the surrounding code related to the error line and make modifications based on this context.\n7. Ensure to observe the currently open file and the current working directory, which is displayed right after the open file. The open file might be in a different directory than the working directory. Remember, commands like \'create\' open files and might alter the current open file.\n8. Effectively using Use search commands (`search_dir`, `search_file`, `find_file`) and navigation commands (`open_file`, `goto_line`) to locate and modify files efficiently. The Editor tool can fully satisfy the requirements. Follow these steps and considerations for optimal results:\n\n9. When the edit fails, try to enlarge the range of code.\n10. # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from odoo import models, fields, api
from odoo.exceptions import ValidationError
from datetime import datetime

class CustomerConsent(models.Model):
    _name = 'customer.consent'
    _description = 'Customer Consent'
    _order = 'consent_date desc'

    user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='User', required=True, index=True)
    consent_given = fields.Boolean(string='Consent Given', required=True, default=False)
    consent_date = fields.Datetime(string='Consent Date', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())
    terms_version = fields.Char(string='Terms & Conditions Version', required=True)
    jurisdiction = fields.Char(string='Jurisdiction', required=True)
    audit_log_ids = fields.One2many('consent.audit', 'consent_id', string='Audit Log')

    @api.model
    def create(self, vals):
        record = super(CustomerConsent, self).create(vals)
        # Log audit entry for consent creation
        self.env['consent.audit'].create({
            'consent_id': record.id,
            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',
            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),
            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,
        })
        return record

    def write(self, vals):
        old_consent_given = self.consent_given
        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)
        # Log audit entry for consent update
        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:
            self.env['consent.audit'].create({
                'consent_id': self.id,
                'action': 'consent_updated',
                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),
                'performed_by': self.env.uid,
            })
        return res

    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):
        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)

class ConsentAudit(models.Model):
    _name = 'consent.audit'
    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'
    _order = 'timestamp desc'

    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)
    action = fields.Selection([
        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),
        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),
        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),
    ], string='Action', required=True)
    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())
    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)

    @api.model
    def create(self, vals):
        # Ensure performed_by is set
        if not vals.get('performed_by'):
            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid
        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)

And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! When editing a file, make sure to insert appropriate indentation before each line! Ensuring the code adheres to PEP8 standards. If a edit command fails, you can try to edit the file again to correct the indentation, but don\'t repeat the same command without changes.\n6. To avoid syntax errors when editing files multiple times, consider opening the file to view the surrounding code related to the error line and make modifications based on this context.\n7. Ensure to observe the currently open file and the current working directory, which is displayed right after the open file. The open file might be in a different directory than the working directory. Remember, commands like \'create\' open files and might alter the current open file.\n8. Effectively using Use search commands (`search_dir`, `search_file`, `find_file`) and navigation commands (`open_file`, `goto_line`) to locate and modify files efficiently. The Editor tool can fully satisfy the requirements. Follow these steps and considerations for optimal results:\n\n9. When the edit fails, try to enlarge the range of code.\n10. You must use the Editor.open_file command to open a file before using the Editor tool\'s edit command to modify it. When you open a file, any currently open file will be automatically closed.\n11. Remember, when you use Editor.insert_content_at_line or Editor.edit_file_by_replace, the line numbers will change after the operation. Therefore, if there are multiple operations, perform only the first operation in the current response, and defer the subsequent operations to the next turn.\n11.1 Do not use Editor.insert_content_at_line or Editor.edit_file_by_replace more than once per command list.\n12. If you choose Editor.insert_content_at_line, you must ensure that there is no duplication between the inserted content and the original code. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from odoo import http, fields, _
from odoo.http import request
from odoo.exceptions import AccessError, UserError

class PortalController(http.Controller):
    @http.route(['/my/portal'], type='http', auth='user', website=True)
    def show_portal(self, **kwargs):
        user = request.env.user
        consent = request.env['customer.consent'].search(
            [('user_id', '=', user.id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1
        )
        values = {
            'user': user,
            'consent': consent,
        }
        return request.render('odoo_online_customer_portal.portal_dashboard', values)

    @http.route(['/my/submit_consent'], type='json', auth='user', methods=['POST'])
    def submit_consent(self, consent_given, terms_version, jurisdiction, **kwargs):
        user = request.env.user
        if not user:
            raise AccessError(_("You must be logged in to submit consent."))
        consent = request.env['customer.consent'].create({
            'user_id': user.id,
            'consent_given': consent_given,
            'terms_version': terms_version,
            'jurisdiction': jurisdiction,
        })
        return {
            'success': True,
            'consent_id': consent.id,
            'message': _("Consent submitted successfully.")
        }

    @http.route(['/my/consent_status'], type='json', auth='user', methods=['GET'])
    def get_consent_status(self, **kwargs):
        user = request.env.user
        consent = request.env['customer.consent'].search(
            [('user_id', '=', user.id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1
        )
        if not consent:
            return {'consent_given': False, 'terms_version': '', 'jurisdiction': '', 'consent_date': ''}
        return {
            'consent_given': consent.consent_given,
            'terms_version': consent.terms_version,
            'jurisdiction': consent.jurisdiction,
            'consent_date': fields.Datetime.to_string(consent.consent_date),
        }

class AdminConsentController(http.Controller):
    @http.route(['/admin/consents'], type='http', auth='user', website=True)
    def list_consents(self, **kwargs):
        if not request.env.user.has_group('base.group_system'):
            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view consents."))
        consents = request.env['customer.consent'].search([], order='consent_date desc')
        values = {
            'consents': consents,
        }
        return request.render('odoo_online_customer_portal.admin_consent_list', values)

    @http.route(['/admin/consent/<int:consent_id>'], type='http', auth='user', website=True)
    def view_consent(self, consent_id, **kwargs):
        if not request.env.user.has_group('base.group_system'):
            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view consent details."))
        consent = request.env['customer.consent'].browse(consent_id)
        if not consent.exists():
            raise UserError(_("Consent record not found."))
        audit_logs = request.env['consent.audit'].search([('consent_id', '=', consent_id)], order='timestamp desc')
        values = {
            'consent': consent,
            'audit_logs': audit_logs,
        }
        return request.render('odoo_online_customer_portal.admin_consent_detail', values)

    @http.route(['/admin/update_consent'], type='json', auth='user', methods=['POST'])
    def update_consent(self, consent_id, consent_given, **kwargs):
        if not request.env.user.has_group('base.group_system'):
            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to update consents."))
        consent = request.env['customer.consent'].browse(consent_id)
        if not consent.exists():
            raise UserError(_("Consent record not found."))
        consent.write({'consent_given': consent_given})
        return {
            'success': True,
            'message': _("Consent updated successfully.")
        }

    @http.route(['/admin/consent_audit/<int:consent_id>'], type='json', auth='user', methods=['GET'])
    def get_audit_trail(self, consent_id, **kwargs):
        if not request.env.user.has_group('base.group_system'):
            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view audit trails."))
        audit_logs = request.env['consent.audit'].search([('consent_id', '=', consent_id)], order='timestamp desc')
        logs = [{
            'action': log.action,
            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.to_string(log.timestamp),
            'performed_by': log.performed_by.name if log.performed_by else '',
        } for log in audit_logs]
        return {'audit_logs': logs}

Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. PortalController(http.Controller):\n    @http.route([\'/my/portal\'], type=\'http\', auth=\'user\', website=True)\n    def show_portal(self, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].search(\n            [(\'user_id\', \'=\', user.id)], order=\'consent_date desc\', limit=1\n        )\n        values = {\n            \'user\': user,\n            \'consent\': consent,\n        }\n        return request.render(\'odoo_online_customer_portal.portal_dashboard\', values)\n\n    @http.route([\'/my/submit_consent\'], type=\'json\', auth=\'user\', methods=[\'POST\'])\n    def submit_consent(self, consent_given, terms_version, jurisdiction, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n        if not user:\n            raise AccessError(_("You must be logged in to submit consent."))\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].create({\n            \'user_id\': user.id,\n            \'consent_given\': consent_given,\n            \'terms_version\': terms_version,\n            \'jurisdiction\': jurisdiction,\n        })\n        return {\n            \'success\': True,\n            \'consent_id\': consent.id,\n            \'message\': _("Consent submitted successfully.")\n        }\n\n    @http.route([\'/my/consent_status\'], type=\'json\', auth=\'user\', methods=[\'GET\'])\n    def get_consent_status(self, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].search(\n            [(\'user_id\', \'=\', user.id)], order=\'consent_date desc\', limit=1\n        )\n        if not consent:\n            return {\'consent_given\': False, \'terms_version\': \'\', \'jurisdiction\': \'\', \'consent_date\': \'\'}\n        return {\n fields.Datetime.to_string(consent.consent_date),\n        }\n\nclass AdminConsentController(http.Controller):\n    @http.route([\'/admin/consents\'], type=\'http\', auth=\'user\', website=True)\n    def list_consents(self, **kwargs):\n        if not request.env.user.has_group(\'base.group_system\'):\n            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view consents."))\n        consents = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].search([], order=\'consent_date desc\')\n        values = {\n            \'consents\': consents,\n        }\n        return view_consent(self, consent_id, **kwargs):\n        if not request.env.user.has_group(\'base.group_system\'):\n            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view consent details."))\n have permission to update consents."))\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].browse(consent_id)\n        if not consent.exists():\n            raise UserError(_("Consent record not Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        #                 </table>

    <!-- Admin Consent Detail Panel -->
    <template id="admin_consent_detail" name="Admin Consent Detail">
        <t t-call="website.layout">
            <div class="o_admin_consent_detail container">
                <h2>Consent Detail</h2>
                <div class="card mb-3">
                    <div class="card-body">
                        <h5 class="card-title"><t t-esc="consent.user_id.name"/></h5>
                        <p class="card-text"><strong>Email:</strong> <t t-esc="consent.user_id.email"/></p>
                        <p class="card-text">
                            <strong>Consent Status:</strong>
                            <span t-if="consent.consent_given" class="badge badge-success">Accepted</span>
                            <span t-if="not consent.consent_given" class="badge badge-danger">Not Accepted</span>
                        </p>
                        <p class="card-text"><strong>Terms Version:</strong> <t t-esc="consent.terms_version"/></p>
                        <p class="card-text"><strong>Jurisdiction:</strong> <t t-esc="consent.jurisdiction"/></p>
                        <p class="card-text"><strong>Date:</strong> <t t-esc="consent.consent_date"/></p>
                        <div class="form-group mt-3">
                            <label for="update_consent_checkbox">Update Consent Status:</label>
                            <input type="checkbox" id="update_consent_checkbox" t-att-checked="consent.consent_given and 'checked' or None"/>
                            <button type="button" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm ml-2" t-att-data-consent_id="consent.id" onclick="updateConsentStatus(this)">
                                Update
                            </button>
                            <span id="update_consent_message" class="ml-2"></span>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <h3>Audit Log</h3>
                <table class="table table-bordered">
                    <thead>
                        <tr>
                            <th>Action</th>
                            <th>Timestamp</th>
                            <th>Performed By</th>
                        </tr>
                    </thead>
                    <tbody>
                        <t t-foreach="audit_logs" t-as="log">
                            <tr>
                                <td><t t-esc="log.action"/></td>
                                <td><t t-esc="log.timestamp"/></td>
                                <td><t t-esc="log.performed_by.name"/></td>
                            </tr>
                        </t>
                    </tbody>
                </table>
            </div>
            <script type="text/javascript">
                function updateConsentStatus(btn) {
                    var consentId = btn.getAttribute('data-consent_id');
                    var consentGiven = document.getElementById('update_consent_checkbox').checked;
                    var messageSpan = document.getElementById('update_consent_message');
                    messageSpan.innerText = '';
                    $.ajax({
                        url: '/admin/update_consent',
                        type: 'POST',
                        contentType: 'application/json',
                        data: JSON.stringify({
                            consent_id: parseInt(consentId),
                            consent_given: consentGiven
                        }),
                        success: function(response) {
                            if (response.success) {
                                messageSpan.innerText = response.message;
                                messageSpan.className = 'text-success ml-2';
                                setTimeout(function(){ location.reload(); }, 1000);
                            } else {
                                messageSpan.innerText = 'Update failed.';
                                messageSpan.className = 'text-danger ml-2';
                            }
                        },
                        error: function() {
                            messageSpan.innerText = 'Error updating consent.';
                            messageSpan.className = 'text-danger ml-2';
                        }
                    });
                }
            </script>
        </t>
    </template>

Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. PortalController(http.Controller):\n    @http.route([\'/my/portal\'], type=\'http\', auth=\'user\', website=True)\n    def show_portal(self, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n consent_given,\n            \'terms_version\': terms_version,\n            \'jurisdiction\': jurisdiction,\n        })\n        return {\n            \'success\': True,\n            \'consent_id\': get_consent_status(self, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].search(\n            [(\'user_id\', \'=\', user.id)], order=\'consent_date def list_consents(self, **kwargs):\n        if not request.env.user.has_group(\'base.group_system\'):\n            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view consents."))\n view_consent(self, consent_id, **kwargs):\n        if not request.env.user.has_group(\'base.group_system\'):\n            raise AccessError(_("You do not have permission to view consent details."))\n Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # /**
 * portal.js - Interactive features for Odoo Online Customer Portal
 * Handles Terms & Conditions checkbox logic, consent form validation,
 * AJAX consent submission, and UI updates for consent status.
 */

odoo.define('odoo_online_customer_portal.portal', function (require) {
    "use strict";

    var ajax = require('web.ajax');
    var core = require('web.core');
    var _t = core._t;

    /**
     * Registration Form: Ensure T&C checkbox is checked before submission.
     */
    $(document).ready(function () {
        // Registration form validation
        var $registrationForm = $('#portal_registration_form');
        if ($registrationForm.length) {
            $registrationForm.on('submit', function (e) {
                var $termsCheckbox = $('#terms_checkbox');
                if (!$termsCheckbox.is(':checked')) {
                    e.preventDefault();
                    alert(_t("You must accept the Terms & Conditions to register."));
                    $termsCheckbox.focus();
                    return false;
                }
            });
        }

        // Consent status AJAX update (for profile/dashboard)
        var $consentStatusContainer = $('.o_portal_consent_status');
        if ($consentStatusContainer.length) {
            ajax.jsonRpc('/my/consent_status', 'call', {})
                .then(function (result) {
                    if (result) {
                        var html = '';
                        if (result.consent_given) {
                            html += '<span class="badge badge-success">' + _t('Accepted') + '</span>';
                        } else {
                            html += '<span class="badge badge-danger">' + _t('Not Accepted') + '</span>';
                        }
                        html += '<br/><strong>' + _t('Terms Version:') + '</strong> ' + result.terms_version;
                        html += '<br/><strong>' + _t('Jurisdiction:') + '</strong> ' + result.jurisdiction;
                        html += '<br/><strong>' + _t('Date:') + '</strong> ' + result.consent_date;
                        $consentStatusContainer.html(html);
                    }
                });
        }

        // Consent submission (if a separate consent form is present)
        var $consentForm = $('#portal_consent_form');
        if ($consentForm.length) {
            $consentForm.on('submit', function (e) {
                e.preventDefault();
                var consentGiven = $('#consent_checkbox').is(':checked');
                var termsVersion = $('#consent_terms_version').val();
                var jurisdiction = $('#consent_jurisdiction').val();

                if (!consentGiven) {
                    alert(_t("You must accept the Terms & Conditions to proceed."));
                    return false;
                }

                ajax.jsonRpc('/my/submit_consent', 'call', {
                    consent_given: consentGiven,
                    terms_version: termsVersion,
                    jurisdiction: jurisdiction
                }).then(function (result) {
                    if (result && result.success) {
                        alert(_t("Consent submitted successfully."));
                        window.location.reload();
                    } else {
                        alert(_t("Failed to submit consent. Please try again."));
                    }
                });
            });
        }

        // Admin: Update consent status via AJAX (handled in QWeb template, but fallback here)
        window.updateConsentStatus = function (btn) {
            var consentId = btn.getAttribute('data-consent_id');
            var consentGiven = document.getElementById('update_consent_checkbox').checked;
            var messageSpan = document.getElementById('update_consent_message');
            messageSpan.innerText = '';
            ajax.jsonRpc('/admin/update_consent', 'call', {
                consent_id: parseInt(consentId),
                consent_given: consentGiven
            }).then(function (response) {
                if (response && response.success) {
                    messageSpan.innerText = response.message;
                    messageSpan.className = 'text-success ml-2';
                    setTimeout(function () { location.reload(); }, 1000);
                } else {
                    messageSpan.innerText = _t('Update failed.');
                    messageSpan.className = 'text-danger ml-2';
                }
            }).catch(function () {
                messageSpan.innerText = _t('Error updating consent.');
                messageSpan.className = 'text-danger ml-2';
            });
        };
    });
});

Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. PortalController(http.Controller):\n    @http.route([\'/my/portal\'], type=\'http\', auth=\'user\', website=True)\n    def show_portal(self, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].search(\n            [(\'user_id\', \'=\', user.id)], order=\'consent_date desc\', limit=1\n        )\n        values = {\n            \'user\': user,\n            \'consent\': consent,\n        }\n        return request.render(\'odoo_online_customer_portal.portal_dashboard\', values)\n\n    @http.route([\'/my/submit_consent\'], type=\'json\', auth=\'user\', methods=[\'POST\'])\n    def submit_consent(self, consent_given, terms_version, jurisdiction, **kwargs):\n        user = request.env.user\n        if not user:\n            raise AccessError(_("You must be logged in to submit consent."))\n        consent = request.env[\'customer.consent\'].create({\n            \'user_id\': user.id,\n            \'consent_given\': consent_given,\n            \'terms_version\': terms_version,\n            \'jurisdiction\': jurisdiction,\n        })\n        return {\n            \'success\': True,\n            \'consent_id\': */\n\nodoo.define(\'odoo_online_customer_portal.portal\', function (require) {\n    "use strict";\n\n    var ajax = require(\'web.ajax\');\n    var core = require(\'web.core\');\n    var _t = core._t;\n\n    /**\n     * Registration Form: Ensure T&C checkbox is checked before submission.\n     */\n    $(document).ready(function () {\n        // Registration form validation\n        var $registrationForm = $(\'#portal_registration_form\');\n        if ($registrationForm.length) {\n            $registrationForm.on(\'submit\', function (e) {\n                var $termsCheckbox = $(\'#terms_checkbox\');\n                if (!$termsCheckbox.is(\':checked\')) {\n                    e.preventDefault();\n                    alert(_t("You must accept the Terms & Conditions to register."));\n                    $termsCheckbox.focus();\n                    return false;\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Consent status AJAX update (for profile/dashboard)\n        var $consentStatusContainer = $(\'.o_portal_consent_status\');\n        if ($consentStatusContainer.length) {\n            ajax.jsonRpc(\'/my/consent_status\', \'call\', {})\n                .then(function (result) {\n                    if (result) {\n                        var html = \'\';\n                        if (result.consent_given) {\n                            html += \'<span class="badge badge-success">\' + _t(\'Accepted\') + \'</span>\';\n                        } else {\n                            html += \'<span class="badge badge-danger">\' + _t(\'Not Accepted\') + \'</span>\';\n                        }\n                        html += \'<br/><strong>\' + _t(\'Terms Version:\') + \'</strong> \' + result.terms_version;\n                        html += \'<br/><strong>\' + _t(\'Jurisdiction:\') + \'</strong> \' + result.jurisdiction;\n                        html += \'<br/><strong>\' + _t(\'Date:\') + \'</strong> \' + result.consent_date;\n                        $consentStatusContainer.html(html);\n                    }\n                });\n        }\n\n        // Consent submission (if a separate consent form is present)\n        var $consentForm = $(\'#portal_consent_form\');\n        if ($consentForm.length) {\n            $consentForm.on(\'submit\', function (e) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                var consentGiven = $(\'#consent_checkbox\').is(\':checked\');\n                var termsVersion = $(\'#consent_terms_version\').val();\n                var jurisdiction = $(\'#consent_jurisdiction\').val();\n\n                if (!consentGiven) {\n                    alert(_t("You must accept the Terms & Conditions to proceed."));\n                    return false;\n                }\n\n                ajax.jsonRpc(\'/my/submit_consent\', \'call\', {\n                    consent_given: consentGiven,\n                    terms_version: termsVersion,\n                    jurisdiction: jurisdiction\n                }).then(function (result) {\n                    if (result && result.success) {\n                        alert(_t("Consent submitted successfully."));\n                        window.location.reload();\n                    } else {\n                        alert(_t("Failed to submit consent. Please try again."));\n                    }\n                });\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Admin: Update consent status via AJAX (handled in QWeb template, but fallback here)\n        window.updateConsentStatus = function (btn) {\n            var consentId = btn.getAttribute(\'data-consent_id\');\n            var consentGiven = document.getElementById(\'update_consent_checkbox\').checked;\n            var messageSpan = document.getElementById(\'update_consent_message\');\n            messageSpan.innerText = \'\';\n            ajax.jsonRpc(\'/admin/update_consent\', \'call\', {\n                consent_id: parseInt(consentId),\n                consent_given: consentGiven\n            }).then(function (response) {\n                if (response && response.success) {\n                    messageSpan.innerText = response.message;\n                    messageSpan.className = \'text-success ml-2\';\n                    setTimeout(function () { location.reload(); }, 1000);\n                } else {\n                    messageSpan.innerText = _t(\'Update failed.\');\n                    messageSpan.className = \'text-danger ml-2\';\n                }\n            }).catch(function () {\n                messageSpan.innerText = _t(\'Error updating consent.\');\n                messageSpan.className = \'text-danger ml-2\';\n            });\n        };\n    });\n});'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'Both portal.scss and portal.js have been successfully implemented for Task 4, covering modern styling and interactive features. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Log audit entry for consent creation\n        self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n            'consent_id': record.id,\n            'action': 'consent_given' if record.consent_given else 'consent_not_given',\n            'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n            'performed_by': record.user_id.id,\n        })\n        return record\n\n    def write(self, vals):\n        old_consent_given = self.consent_given\n        res = super(CustomerConsent, self).write(vals)\n        # Log audit entry for consent update\n        if 'consent_given' in vals and vals['consent_given'] != old_consent_given:\n            self.env['consent.audit'].create({\n                'consent_id': self.id,\n                'action': 'consent_updated',\n                'timestamp': fields.Datetime.now(),\n                'performed_by': self.env.uid,\n            })\n        return res\n\n    def get_latest_consent(self, user_id):\n        return self.search([('user_id', '=', user_id)], order='consent_date desc', limit=1)\n\nclass ConsentAudit(models.Model):\n    _name = 'consent.audit'\n    _description = 'Consent Audit Log'\n    _order = 'timestamp desc'\n\n    consent_id = fields.Many2one('customer.consent', string='Consent', required=True, index=True)\n    action = fields.Selection([\n        ('consent_given', 'Consent Given'),\n        ('consent_not_given', 'Consent Not Given'),\n        ('consent_updated', 'Consent Updated'),\n    ], string='Action', required=True)\n    timestamp = fields.Datetime(string='Timestamp', required=True, default=lambda self: fields.Datetime.now())\n    performed_by = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Performed By', required=True)\n\n    @api.model\n    def create(self, vals):\n        # Ensure performed_by is set\n        if not vals.get('performed_by'):\n            vals['performed_by'] = self.env.uid\n        return super(ConsentAudit, self).create(vals)"}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of models/customer_consent.py is complete, defining both CustomerConsent and ConsentAudit models according to the requirements. Online Customer Portal\n * Handles Terms & Conditions checkbox logic, consent form validation,\n * AJAX consent submission, and UI updates for consent status.\n */\n\nodoo.define(\'odoo_online_customer_portal.portal\', function (require) {\n    "use strict";\n\n    var ajax = require(\'web.ajax\');\n    var core = require(\'web.core\');\n    var _t = core._t;\n\n    /**\n     * Registration Form: Ensure T&C checkbox is checked before submission.\n     */\n    $(document).ready(function () {\n        // Registration form validation\n        var $registrationForm = $(\'#portal_registration_form\');\n        if ($registrationForm.length) {\n            $registrationForm.on(\'submit\', function (e) {\n                var $termsCheckbox = $(\'#terms_checkbox\');\n                if (!$termsCheckbox.is(\':checked\')) {\n                    e.preventDefault();\n                    alert(_t("You must accept the Terms & Conditions to _t(\'Not Accepted\') + \'</span>\';\n                        }\n                        html += \'<br/><strong>\' + _t(\'Terms Version:\') + \'</strong> \' + result.terms_version;\n                        html += \'<br/><strong>\' + _t(\'Jurisdiction:\') + \'</strong> \' + result.jurisdiction;\n                        html += \'<br/><strong>\' + _t(\'Date:\') + \'</strong> \' + $(\'#consent_jurisdiction\').val();\n\n                if (!consentGiven) {\n                    alert(_t("You must accept the Terms & Conditions to proceed."));\n                    return false;\n                }\n\n                ajax.jsonRpc(\'/my/submit_consent\', \'call\', {\n                    consent_given: consentGiven,\n                    terms_version: termsVersion,\n                    jurisdiction: jurisdiction\n                }).then(function (result) {\n                    if (result && result.success) {\n                        alert(_t("Consent submitted successfully."));\n                        window.location.reload();\n                    } else {\n                        alert(_t("Failed to submit consent. Please try again."));\n                    }\n                });\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Admin: Update consent status via AJAX (handled in QWeb template, but fallback here)\n        window.updateConsentStatus = function (btn) {\n            var consentId = btn.getAttribute(\'data-consent_id\');\n            var consentGiven = document.getElementById(\'update_consent_checkbox\').checked;\n            var messageSpan = document.getElementById(\'update_consent_message\');\n            messageSpan.innerText = \'\';\n            ajax.jsonRpc(\'/admin/update_consent\', \'call\', {\n                consent_id: parseInt(consentId),\n                consent_given: consentGiven\n            }).then(function (response) {\n                if (response && response.success) {\n                    messageSpan.innerText = response.message;\n                    messageSpan.className = \'text-success ml-2\';\n                    setTimeout(function () { location.reload(); }, 1000);\n                } else {\n                    messageSpan.innerText = _t(\'Update failed.\');\n                    messageSpan.className = \'text-danger ml-2\';\n                }\n            }).catch(function () {\n                messageSpan.innerText = _t(\'Error updating consent.\');\n                messageSpan.className = \'text-danger ml-2\';\n            });\n        };\n    });\n});'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'Both portal.scss and portal.js have been successfully implemented for Task 4, covering modern styling and interactive features.
